headline: SanDisk’s Latest Results Reset Expectations Again
summary: Here’s a number that makes you look twice: in a single quarter, SanDisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) generated nearly $3 billion in free cash flow, posted EPS of $23.41 vs estimates of $14.62, and then guided the next quarter to as much as $8.25 billion in revenue. About a year ago, the stock was at $40. Now it’s around $1,400. That’s not hype, it’s timing, execution, and a massive shift in demand all lining up. See, SanDisk’s Surge Explained: What’s Fueling the Move.
